candidates.why: Explicit COGS-related depreciation line, but it appears directly beneath the selected COGS bucket with a corresponding balancing row, indicating it is already embedded in the selected 'Cost of goods sold' / 'Cost of Revenues/COGS' total rather than sitting outside it.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles including Goodwill in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: Explicit COGS-related amortization label, but shown as a component within cost of revenues; also values are blank in the preview. This suggests no separate add-back row should be taken relative to the selected COGS total.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles excluding Goodwill in Cost of Revenues
candidates.why: Explicit COGS-related amortization line, but it sits with the COGS breakdown and balancing row, indicating it is already contained in the selected COGS total.

variables.notes: The selected COGS reference bucket is the reported COGS total ('Cost of goods sold' / 'Cost of Revenues/COGS'). The depreciation/amortization rows shown under cost of revenues appear to be sub-components of that total, evidenced by the accompanying 'Cost for goods sold - Balancing value' row. Adding them separately would double count.

candidates.why: Explicit SG&A-related depreciation, but it appears directly beneath 'Sales expenses' with a balancing row, indicating it is already included in the selected parent row.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles excluding Goodwill in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Explicit SG&A-related amortization, but it is presented as part of the sales/administrative expense breakdown and appears already embedded in the selected SG&A component rows. Also values are zero in most periods.
Explicit admin-related depreciation, but it sits directly beneath 'Administrative expenses' with a balancing row, indicating inclusion in the selected parent row.
Separate operating line and not clearly a pure SG&A D&A row; it mixes amortization with other comparability items, so using it as XSGA_DA risks misclassification and double counting.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA bucket is the sum of 'Sales expenses' and 'Administrative expenses'. The visible depreciation/amortization rows under those headings appear to be internal components already included in those selected rows, as indicated by the associated balancing rows. 'Amortizations and items affecting comparability' is not used because it is not clearly a pure SG&A D&A line.
